title: specpilot config
description: Configuration for the specpilot cogloop plugin

owner.description: GitHub org or username that owns the project board

projectNumber.description: GitHub Project number (from the project URL)

assignee.description: Optional: only pick tickets assigned to this GitHub username

repos.description: All repos involved in the workflow. Add as many as your project needs.

name.description: Repo name used in PR titles and comments

path.description: Relative path to the repo (or folder) from the project root

role.description: 'spec' = where speckit writes artifacts (only one allowed). 'feature' = any repo that gets implemented (frontend, backend, mobile, infra, etc.)

implement.description: Whether speckit should implement code changes in this repo. Default: true for feature repos. Set to false for repos that only need a PR (e.g. docs, config).

test.description: Command to run tests (e.g. 'npm test'). null = skip testing.

build.description: Command to run build (e.g. 'npm run build'). null = skip build.

waitForCI.description: Whether to wait for CI checks to pass before merging the PR. Auto-detected during install based on presence of .github/workflows/. Default: false.

specsDir.description: Path to the spec directory — either a separate repo (e.g. '../my-spec') or a folder inside the project (e.g. './spec'). speckit writes all artifacts here. May contain app subfolders (e.g. 'member/', 'admin/') for multi-app projects; specpilot detects these automatically.

workspaceDir.description: Path to the directory where speckit slash commands are available (where CLAUDE.md with speckit commands lives). Usually the project root '.' or parent '..'.

statuses.description: GitHub Project column names — must match exactly what's on your board
